data.title: Praetorian prices $220M initial public offering
data.description:
• Praetorian Acquisition (PTORU) said on Thursday it has priced its initial public offering of 22M  at $10 per unit, raising $220M, with the shares expected to begin trading on Jan. 23, 2026, with underwriters granted a 45-day option to purchase up to 3.3M additional units.
  • Each unit consists of one Class A ordinary share and one-third of a redeemable warrant, with each whole warrant exercisable to purchase one Class A ordinary share at $11.50.
  • No fractional warrants will be issued upon unit separation, and only whole warrants will trade.
  • The company is led by CEO Justin Di Rezze and CFO Peter Ondishin, both members of the Board, which also includes Nicole Seligman, Alex Elias, and Erica Dorfman.
  • The offering is expected to close on January 26, 2026.

data.title: Capital Southwest launches first-out senior loan joint venture
data.description:
Capital Southwest (CSWC)  formed a joint venture with another private credit asset manager to invest primarily in first-out senior secured debt opportunities in the lower middle-market, the company said on Thursday.

The joint venture will be owned equally by Capital Southwest (CSWC) and its joint venture partner, with each holding a 50% equity interest. All investment and operational decisions for the fund will be made by the joint venture’s board of managers, which will consist of equal representation from both joint venture partners.

The company expects the joint venture will fund investments through a senior secured credit facility it's expected to enter.

"We believe the creation of this joint venture will enhance CSWC’s ability to compete for and win high‑quality lower middle market opportunities by providing more flexible capital solutions," said CSWC CEO Michael Sarner. "It also will allow CSWC to allocate portions of larger transactions to the joint venture, enabling us to maintain portfolio granularity while broadening the range of platform companies we can pursue in the lower middle market."

Capital Southwest (CSWC) stock rose 0.7% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Western Exploration prices C$8M brokered bought-deal offering
data.description:
• Western Exploration (WEX:CA) announced on Thursday that it has agreed with Paradigm Capital, acting as lead agent and sole bookrunner, on a C$8M private placement under the LIFE exemption.
  • The offering comprises 8.7M units priced at C$0.92 each, for gross proceeds of about C$8.0M. 
  • Each unit will consist of one variable voting share and one-half of one share purchase warrant, with each whole warrant exercisable to acquire one variable voting share at C$1.35 within 24 months of the closing date.
  • Warrants become callable if the stock’s 20-day volume-weighted average exceeds C$1.70 at any time starting six months after closing and before warrant expiry.
  • The agents have been granted an option to sell up to an additional 1.08M units at the offering price for additional gross proceeds of up to C$0.93M. 
  • Net proceeds will be used to fund exploration and advancement of its Aura project in Nevada, as well as for general corporate purposes and working capital.
  • The offering is scheduled to close on February 3, 2026.

data.title: Pinnacle Financial targets 9%–11% loan growth and $5–5.2B revenue in 2026 as Synovus merger integration accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Pinnacle Financial Partners, Inc. (PNFP) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Kevin Blair, President and CEO, emphasized the newly combined company's commitment to "producing strong above peer revenue, earnings per share and tangible book value growth." He highlighted that the focus remains on exceptional client service and a collaborative culture, stating, "These priorities enable us to attract and retain revenue producers at an outsized pace, fueling our continued growth."
  • Blair detailed the successful merger with Synovus, completed January 1, only 160 days after announcement, noting, "Both organizations have successfully completed key milestones." He described swift integration efforts, including incorporating legacy Synovus team members into Pinnacle’s operating rhythm and sales meetings.
  • The company's 2025 performance was strong, with legacy Pinnacle growing adjusted diluted EPS by 22% and legacy Synovus by 28%. Blair said, "Our team is fully engaged, focused on our clients and delivering meaningful value for our shareholders."
  • Andrew Gregory, Executive VP & CFO, reported, "Pinnacle reported fourth quarter adjusted EPS of $2.24, which was stable quarter-over-quarter and up 18% from the prior year. Net interest income increased 3% from the third quarter and 12% year-over-year."
  • Gregory also noted, "Net charge-offs were contained at $27 million or 28 basis points, 63% of which was from a single non-owner-occupied CRE loan. The CET1 ratio ended the quarter at 10.88%."

Outlook

  • Blair projected, "We expect our period-end loans to grow to $91 billion to $93 billion or up 9% to 11% versus our combined loans at year-end 2025."
  • Total deposits are expected to reach $106.5 billion to $108.5 billion, up 8% to 10% in 2026.
  • The adjusted revenue outlook for 2026 is $5 billion to $5.2 billion. Blair stated, "The net interest margin is estimated in the 3.45% to 3.55% range."
  • Adjusted non-interest revenue is anticipated at $1.1 billion, with BHG investment income contributing approximately $125 million to $135 million.
  • Adjusted non-interest expense is forecasted at $2.7 billion to $2.8 billion in 2026, with a target of realizing 40% or $100 million of annualized merger-related expense savings.
  • Net charge-offs are estimated in the range of 20 to 25 basis points for the year. The CET1 ratio is targeted at 10.25% to 10.75%. The quarterly common equity dividend will be $0.50 per share, and a $400 million share repurchase program has been authorized.

Financial Results

  • Net interest income increased 3% from the third quarter and 12% year-over-year for Pinnacle.
  • Period-end loans grew 3% from the prior quarter and 10% year-over-year. Core deposit growth was also 3% quarter-over-quarter and 10% year-over-year.
  • The net interest margin increased to 3.27%. Adjusted non-interest revenue declined 6% from the third quarter but rose 25% year-over-year. BHG contributed $31 million in fee revenue for Pinnacle.
  • Synovus reported fourth quarter adjusted diluted EPS of $1.45, stable quarter-over-quarter and up 16% year-over-year. Net interest income increased 2% quarter-over-quarter and 7% year-over-year, with period-end loan growth of $872 million, or 2% from the prior quarter.
  • Synovus’ net interest margin reached 3.45% and adjusted non-interest revenue grew 6% from the prior quarter and 16% year-over-year.
  • Both firms hired 41 new revenue producers in the fourth quarter, totaling 217 for 2025.

Q&A

  • Ebrahim Poonawala, BofA Securities, asked about merger systems conversion and new banker onboarding. Blair responded that both companies are operating on legacy platforms until the 2027 conversion, but "it's not stopping our ability to grow the business."
  • Poonawala followed up about the $400 million buyback. Gregory answered, "It's unlikely you would see them in the second quarter, but then we will reassess as we get into -- later into the year."
  • John Pancari, Evercore, inquired about loan growth confidence. Blair cited, "we generated 10% loan growth already" in Q4 and expressed confidence in achieving 9% to 11% due to hiring and specialty business expansion.
  • Pancari asked about cost saves; Gregory explained, "those 2 things really drove the 50% down to 40% on the year 1 cost saves...it's really a timing difference."
  • Jared Shaw, Barclays, asked about fee income guidance for Capital Markets. Gregory highlighted "over 10% growth in account analysis fees...over 15% growth in swap fees," with expectations for double-digit growth in 2026.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts exhibited a constructive but probing tone, focusing on merger integration, growth outlook, cost saves, and competitive positioning.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently using phrases such as "we are confident" and "I am more excited than ever."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone has shifted to emphasize operational integration and future growth, while analyst sentiment remained attentive but slightly more focused on execution risk.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Current quarter brought detailed post-merger integration updates and explicit forward guidance for the combined entity, compared to the prior quarter’s focus on standalone results and merger anticipation.
  • Loan growth guidance increased from 9% to 10% (previous quarter) to 9% to 11% (current quarter) for the combined firm.
  • Share repurchase and dividend policies were outlined more explicitly in the current quarter.
  • Analysts shifted from merger mechanics and hiring momentum to integration execution, cost synergies, and specialty business growth.
  • Management confidence remained high, but now with a greater emphasis on merger execution and delivering on promised synergies.

Risks and Concerns

  • Blair acknowledged "ongoing challenges, especially as we prepare for conversion in the first quarter of 2027," but emphasized readiness.
  • Analysts queried the timing and realization of cost synergies, with management attributing delays to rapid deal closure and system integration timing.
  • Credit risk was discussed, with net charge-offs mainly attributed to a single CRE loan, and guidance for stability in charge-off levels.
  • The outlook incorporates potential headwinds from economic uncertainty and competitive hiring/retention dynamics.

Final Takeaway

Management highlighted that 2026 marks the first full year as a combined company, with clear targets for loan and deposit growth, merger-related expense savings, and robust revenue expansion. The successful integration with Synovus, continued strong hiring momentum, and a focus on operational excellence position Pinnacle Financial to achieve its growth and profitability objectives, while remaining attentive to integration, credit, and competitive risks in the evolving market landscape.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: New York regulator slashes ConEd’s rate request by more than 85%, citing affordability
data.description:
The New York State Public Service Commission said Thursday it adopted the terms of an agreement between Consolidated Edison (ED), consumer advocates and other parties that would limit the utility's revenues and cap increases to customer bills for the next three years.

The deal will raise Con Ed's (ED) revenues by $234M in the first year and more than $400M in each of the next two years, while capping increases in customer bills at the rate of inflation, the regulator said.

While the PSC's action lowers the company's request for total electric delivery revenues by more than $1.37B in the first year, an 87% decrease from the initial request, it still reflects a return on equity of 9.4%.

"The three-year rate plan... is a forward-looking plan that benefits customers and includes provisions that further important state and commission objectives, while keeping customer affordability first and foremost in mind," the PSC said.

Con Ed (ED) shares closed -0.6% on Thursday after falling as much as 2.5%.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase's Dimon earns $43M in 2025, up 10.2% Y/Y
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon's compensation climbed 10.2% Y/Y to $43M in 2025, reflecting persistent strength across the bank's businesses and financials, as well as its balance sheet, it disclosed on Thursday.

His annual pay includes an annual base salary of $1.50M and performance-based variable incentive compensation of $41.5M, according to a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission filing.

Some $5.00M of the variable incentive compensation will be delivered in cash, and the remaining $36,500,000 will be received in the form of at-risk performance share units.

Under Dimon's leadership, the company logged record revenue in 2025 for the eighth straight year while also boosting its dividend, it said. Also during the year, JPMorgan (JPM) raised about $3.3T of credit and capital for its consumer and institutional clients globally, and it continued its investments in AI research and capabilities, data, and technology.

data.title: OceanFirst Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $0.41 beats by $0.05, revenue of $104.6M beats by $0.9M
data.description:
• OceanFirst Financial press release (OCFC): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.41 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $104.6M (+9.5% Y/Y) beats by $0.9M.

data.title: Capital One Q4 earnings miss as credit loss provision, expenses climb
data.description:
Capital One Financial (COF) stock dipped 4.1% in Thursday after-hours trading after the credit card company posted weaker-than-expected Q4 earnings as expenses and its provision for credit losses increased. Comparisons to year-ago results reflect the company's acquisition of Discover Financial in May 2025.

The company also announced that it agreed to acquire fintech Brex for $5.15B.

Q4 adjusted EPS of $3.86, trailing the average analyst estimate of $4.14, fell from $5.95 in Q3 and $3.09 in Q4 2024.

During the quarter, Capital One (COF) recorded:

  • A $509M intangible amortization expense on Discover, 
  • $352M of Discover integration expenses,
  • $37M Discover loan and deposit fair value mark amortization, and
  • Legal reserve of $117M
  • Those are partly offset by a $483M gain on the sale of its home loan portfolio and
  • The reversal of $29M on its FDIC special assessment. 

That brought its Q4 GAAP EPS to $3.26, down from $4.83 in Q3 and up from $2.67 in Q4 2024.

Total revenue of $15.6B, beating the $15.5B consensus, increased from $15.4B in the prior quarter and $10.2B a year ago.

Net interest income of $12.5B, exceeding the Visible Alpha consensus of $12.4B, climbed from $12.4B in Q3 and $8.10B in Q4 2024. Its net interest margin of 8.26% narrowed from 8.36% in the previous quarter and grew from 7.03% a year ago. The Y/Y increase reflects its Discover acquisition. Adjusted net interest margin of 8.28% narrowed from 8.43% in the prior quarter and widened from 7.03% a year ago.

Q4 preprovision earnings of $6.24B, missing the Visible Alpha estimate of $6.80B, declined from $7.10B in Q3 and increased from $4.10B in the prior year’s Q4.

Provision for credit losses of $4.14B, exceeding the Visible Alpha estimate of $4.09B, jumped from $2.71B in the previous quarter and $2.64B a year ago.

Net charge-off rate of 3.45% vs. 3.16% in Q3 and 3.59% in the year-ago Q4.

Noninterest expense totaled $9.34B, up from $8.26B in the prior quarter and $6.09B a year ago.

Total deposits of $475.78B at Dec. 31, 2025, increased from $468.8B at Sept. 30. Loans held for investment were $453.6B at Dec. 31, 2025, vs. $443.2B at Sept. 30.

"Years of strategic preparation and our choices to consistently invest to sustain long-term growth and returns enable our results and put us in a strong position going forward," said Founder, Chairman, and CEO Richard D. Fairbank.

Conference call at 5:00 PM ET.

data.title: Glacier Bancorp GAAP EPS of $0.49 in-line, revenue of $306.5M beats by $0.63M
data.description:

data.title: AMC Networks CFO Patrick O’Connell to depart
data.description:
• AMC Networks  (AMCX) said on Thursday that CFO, Patrick O’Connell will leave the company.
  • O’Connell will remain with the company through March and is expected to participate in the Q4 and FY 2025 earnings call.
  • The company has initiated a search for O’Connell’s successor.
